recentReviews.text:
This will amuse you. I didn't pass the technical inspection because the manufacturer's label on the body was slightly peeled off (the VIN is still legible).
And at Auto Jarov they don't hesitate to charge 5200,- for the label and another 800,- for sticking it on!!
The production price is around 20 CZK.
That's a pretty good markup, 29900%

Stick-on takes 2 minutes = 120 seconds, i.e. they charge 7 CZK per second of work. 🤣 so if their technician takes a 5-minute break for a cigarette, VW is immediately at a loss of 2000 CZK.

It's crap. And then the car companies complain about how hard it is.

To make the service even more difficult, I unmistakably made an appointment for Jarov, but put the car in the keybox in Kunratice. Just a scoundrel :-). But here Mr. Svoboda came "on me" and, lo and behold... Miracles do happen. I thank you very much and highly recommend it (there were several problems, but everything was resolved to my satisfaction). Unfortunately, I did not receive a feedback form, so I am sending a public review. It's all about people!

recentReviews.text: A big disappointment for me. On October 11th, I sent an inquiry for an advertised Škoda Superb offered on Škoda plus. A few hours later, an Auto Strž employee called me, informed me about the car and how it was, and answered my questions, and everything was fine, so I arranged to book the car and make an appointment for October 18th, and he agreed. On the day of the appointment, I called 30 minutes in advance to say that I was on my way and the inspection was confirmed. When I entered the property, an employee of the company came to me and willingly showed me the car, when I had inspected it and wanted to drive it, which I was allowed to do. After the drive, I showed obvious interest in the car, and the employee asked me to be patient and ran to the back office. After returning, he told me that his colleague allegedly did not know about my interest (which was the whole week and there were three of them at the branch) and the car was booked by some company on Tuesday, October 15th, which made a deposit and was issued an invoice, so the car is theoretically no longer there. That made me very angry and a bit upset, but I somehow came to terms with it. Well, I couldn't help it and after some time I looked at other advertisements than on Škoda plus, where the car was advertised (it has not been there since October 15th) and I found an advertisement for this car on the pages of another portal, where it is advertised, with the only difference being that it is CZK 90,000 more expensive than the one originally offered on Škoa plus. So for me, it is a complete disgrace, unless it is a mistake!!!

WARNING, WARNING – scam!

I came to have my chassis and central locking repaired. During the repair, they noticed that the brake pads on the wheels needed to be replaced. They quoted me a TOTAL of approximately 18,000 CZK over the phone. Two days later, however, I received a message that they wanted a total of approximately 24,000 CZK.

When I arrived to pick up the car, the colleague who had quoted me the repair over the phone was not there at the time – very convenient… The manager tried to contact him but could not. They only gave me a choice: either remove the old brakes or pay, so they did not solve the situation at all.

In short – scammers. I would NEVER, NEVER, NEVER go there to have my car repaired again and I would recommend everyone to avoid this place.

recentReviews.text: Great dissatisfaction with the service. Classic oil, pollen filter, brake fluid change. Price agreed to 6000 CZK. The car was supposed to be ready in a few hours, a little while later I got a phone call saying that the front shock absorbers needed to be replaced, that one of them had leaked (nothing terrible, since I saw the shock absorber when I took it over), the car drove to the service, braked, and held the road fine, after all, it was only a 5.5 year old car with 82 thousand kilometers on the clock, the shock absorbers should be half their lifespan) + rear shock absorber stops. When I asked how much it would cost in total (the word total is important), I was told that it would be 17 thousand. I agreed because I expected it to be about 6000 for oil, 11,000 for shock absorbers. Unfortunately, the car had to be in the service until the next day, which greatly complicated my plans, but when it is there, it will be done all at once. (a bit surprising that such a part is not in stock for such a common brand model) The next day I get a text message with a total amount of 22,300 CZK. When I ask about the total amount I will have to pay there, I expect an answer with the amount I will pay there via card. Unfortunately, no one else is going with me to pay the rest. But the shock absorbers have been replaced, so it is difficult to complain about anything now. I do not record calls. I took over the car, fortunately I managed to stop the car being washed, which was a gift without me asking for anything like that or being informed about anything. After taking over the car, I was very disappointed. For this high amount, the car floats, does not hold, the brakes are weaker (which is really strange when they did not do them), you can feel every small unevenness and what was the absolute highlight, that after starting it there was some radio playing, which I never turn on in the car, I do not enjoy listening to commercials. The steering wheel was dirty (another authorized service always used a steering wheel cover and not just the seat. Unfortunately, I can't give half a star, the service would have taken that away for being the only one in Prague where you can bring your own oil. Fortunately, this was my last visit. Unfortunately, I was disappointed by the Škoda brand overall. This is the third Škoda car I've owned, but after this experience I'll probably choose another brand when buying, just not the group including Škoda.

I've been a loyal customer of Auto Klokočka for quite some time now, and I must say, they never fail to impress me. The level of service and professionalism they provide is all that I look for as a client.

Every time I've brought in my cars for servicing, the team at Auto Klokočka has gone above and beyond to ensure that everything is taken care of promptly and efficiently. Their additional services, such as car pick-up, make the whole process incredibly convenient.

What sets Auto Klokočka apart is not just their speed of repairs, but also the friendly and knowledgeable staff who always greet you with a smile.

Recently, I had the pleasure of selling one of my cars through their buyout program, and I couldn't be happier with the service I received. Mr. Tomáš Chýle, in particular, demonstrated remarkable expertise and professionalism throughout the entire process. His fair price negotiations and expert assessment were truly impressive and he is creating a great reputation for himself and for the company too.

I highly recommend Auto Klokočka to anyone in need of car servicing or looking to buy or sell a vehicle. You can trust them to provide top-notch service and expertise every step of the way.

Thank you, Auto Klokočka, for consistently exceeding my expectations!

recentReviews.text:
I would put 5 stars but I think there is room for improvement.

The buying experience went great, a seller spoke to me in english and offered me a test drive, then a clerk took me in for signing and explaining administrative procedures.

I would recommend to put attention in a specific document you have to sign where a more detailed description on the current state of the vehicle is indicated.

I did not bring a mechanic and I didn't specially look at this document as there are several documents that needed to be signed on the tablet the clerk handed me, while at the same time I was looking for the best insurance option to take the car out of the bazar.

Unfortunately, my car broke down a few days after... It was starting to be a nightmare because the technical claim department was not approving the repairs I needed to make on the car, at this point the document I signed (mentioned above) played an important role.

I was giving up, thinking to find a lawyer to initiate legal procedures, changing mechanic for second opinion, preparing a more aggressive email for the technical-claim department, all these ideas were there, but I CALMED DOWN, I didn’t change my mechanic, I didn't call a lawyer, instead I wrote a polite and sincere email to the technician from the claims office attaching the latest more in-depth technical review that my mechanic wrote of the car and then I was offered to change the vehicle, which ended up to be the option which solved everything .

I came back to the dealership, all administrative procedures went really smoothly and I got a new car the same day, it's been almost 3 months since then and the car works great, I have been taking it to the highway very often as it is the reason why I needed it and I can say there's no complications at all.

I recommend to consider auto ESA but I also recommend to bring someone who can do a technical review on the spot, and to read that specific document that describes the mechanical condition of the car, as a plus it will be great if you can take the car out to a highway so to be sure it will perform well above 80km/h

recentReviews.text:
RESPONSE TO Auto Jarov:

After 1.5 hours of paid diagnostics, done without removing any parts, you claimed that internal DSG components were damaged and recommended replacing the entire gearbox, which is one of the most expensive parts of the vehicle.

Saying that “procedures were followed” does not change the fact that the diagnosis was completely wrong. The car was fully repaired by replacing a faulty drive shaft, which you had clearly stated was “completely fine”. This is not a different opinion or repair method, it proves your diagnosis was incorrect.

The issue is not that the car was repaired elsewhere or outside your network. The issue is that after 1.5 hours of "looking" at the car, an official Volkswagen service recommended an unnecessary 250,000 CZK gearbox replacement. This seriously raises questions about diagnostic quality, professional judgment, and responsibility at an authorized service center.

Short Summary of the review:
Auto Jarov completely misdiagnosed my VW T6 Multivan and insisted that the vehicle required a full DSG replacement costing over 250,000 CZK. This diagnosis was wrong. A second mechanic later confirmed that the real issue was only a faulty drive shaft, which was repaired for 35,000 CZK. Their mistake cost me unnecessary expenses and left my family without a car for more than a month. Even after being confronted with the correct diagnosis, Auto Jarov's service manager refused to admit fault and offered only a partial refund, attempting to justify an obvious error. This behavior seriously undermines trust in an official Volkswagen service center and raises concerns about their competence and ethics

Below is a long review, but I believe the details matter:
About a month and a half ago, our VW T6 Multivan broke down and was towed by insurance to Auto Jarov. At the time of the breakdown, I contacted the VW mechanic who had previously serviced the vehicle. He told me that the most likely cause was a faulty drive shaft and advised me to check that first. I shared this exact information with Auto Jarov before they performed their diagnostic and explicitly asked them to inspect the drive shaft as a priority.

After a few hours, Auto Jarov contacted me and stated that the issue was a failure of the differential in the DSG transmission. They confirmed that the drive shaft had been checked and was “completely fine.” I was also told that they do not repair individual DSG components and that the only solution was a full DSG replacement, with parts alone costing approximately 250,000 CZK, excluding labor.

I made it clear that I was not willing to spend roughly one-third of the car’s value on this repair and asked once again whether they were absolutely certain about the diagnosis. They confirmed that they were. I was then charged for 1.5 hours of diagnostic work in the amount of 2,759 CZK, which I paid.

I had the car towed to my parking and began searching for an alternative solution. I eventually found a refurbished DSG unit in Germany and ordered it, and I took the car to another mechanic for installation. Before replacing the DSG, this mechanic diagnosed the car and found that the problem was in fact only a faulty drive shaft. After replacing the drive shaft, the car worked perfectly.
In total, instead of paying over 250,000 CZK, I paid approximately 35,000 CZK for parts, labor, full service, DSG service, and a tire change.
I contacted Auto Jarov to inform them of the incorrect diagnosis and requested a full refund of the diagnostic invoice. Their response was that their diagnosis was done properly and that they were willing to refund only 50%, stating they had made “only half of a mistake.”

For me, this is not about the money, it is about principle. When I go to an official Volkswagen dealership, I expect expertise, transparency, and accountability. Instead, I experienced a clear misdiagnosis, a refusal to accept responsibility, and an attempt to minimize an obvious error. Because of this, I will formally contact Volkswagen and push this matter through official channels.

recentReviews.text:
Terrible , unprofessional and outright should be stripped of their license. Making a very bad name for VW.
I came to change a cracked door glass under the warranty of a brand new car. The technicians were rude and unhelpful, even actively trying to deceive me so that they don't have to file a report and service the vehicle.
The first hand response: "go fix your car where you bought it". One would assume that a certified service can handle a car that was bought 500km away two weeks ago. Apparently AUTO JAROV can not :/ After brainstorming with 4 different employees on what should they do and wasting an hour of my time, they finally agreed to go take a look at the window.
"Thorough inspection by an expert" in AUTO JAROV constituted a badly mannered man that came to a window and chipped a piece of shard from the glass with his nail and proclaimed: "It's from a rock, that's not covered under warranty". Even my 5 year old daughter understands that a small rock cannot crack the window in such a way to create multiple cracks from underneath the drivers door.
There were no attempts to remove the door and take a look at the root of the crack which was hidden under the door so a rock couldn't have caused it. Which should be a standard way of dealing with such cases, I assumed they are just incompetent, but they clearly made attempts to decieve, make me loose my patience, leave and never come again so they don't have to file a report and disassemble the door and replace the window(which worked so props to them) .
I would rather drive 30km to a different dealership next time. So if you don't want to feel terrible during your visit, go to a different place...
UPDATE: I went to another dealership,  technicians were actually helpful, removed the window in 90 minutes and confirmed it's a factory error and is under warranty, and then replaced it.

recentReviews.text: The service at the showroom is absolutely horrible to say the least. Rude and arrogant behavior from the sales people we came across, on two separate occasions. The first time was a few months ago when we went to their shop for a test drive, which was previously booked a week in advance over the phone. When we got to the shop on the day of the test drive, they came up with a lame excuse that they were not doing any test drives on that day. But of course nobody bothered to call or write us before we went there and tell us this. The second time, was today, when we were really interested in buying a car which they had in their showroom according to their website. Of course again, before visiting their shop we called them a few days in advance to make sure that the car is still available and also book a date and time to inspect it and quite possibly go ahead and buy it. There was not even a need to test-drive the car as we had already test-driven the exact same model, with different extras, at a different dealership a few weeks before. This would have been very likely an open and shut deal for both us and them, as the car specs and the price of the car were ideal for us. Alas, to our complete disappointment we went to the dealership, only to be greeted by a blond lady, who was extremely rude and obnoxious, asking us repeatedly if we have an appointment, as if that would have changed our original answer. This was a person who seemed to be bothered by us and our interest in buying a car from the very same business she is employed by! So, first she claimed that the car is not available anymore, then she claimed that her colleague, to whom we talked on the phone, confused the cars (even though there was only one car that could  fit that description at their shop), then she claimed that the car (which is suppose to be a showroom car !!!) is not currently available because is used by the dealership employees (then why are you advertising it on your website?) and finally she claimed that the car could not be purchased for the next three months (again no information about this on their website). An absolute lack of professionalism, expertise and basic good manners. If you are interested in buying a Skoda, there are plenty of good dealerships in and around of Prague which you can visit and find a suitable deal for you. This is definitely not one of them!

recentReviews.text:
I represent GENTLEAN 156, a French company specializing in automotive consulting and vehicle sourcing for discerning clients.

I recently imported an Audi Q3 Sportback from the Czech Republic for one of my clients, using the CarBuzz24 platform. The vehicle was purchased from V-Auto ZEBRAK S.R.O.
Overall, the transaction went very smoothly. The car was delivered in perfect condition, exactly as described, and within the expected timeframe. I truly appreciated the professionalism shown throughout the process.

Unfortunately, one important detail was overlooked. The vehicle was advertised as being equipped with Apple CarPlay and Android Auto. However, upon delivery, these features were not available.

I had to take the car to an Audi dealership in France to have the system updated and these features activated, at an additional cost of €800.
Despite several follow-ups, I have received no response from the seller regarding this issue.

It’s a shame, as everything else went very well. I hope this feedback helps improve the accuracy of future listings and post-sale support.

John Michelin
CEO – GENTLEAN 156

recentReviews.text: Drupol Zdice - never again. In the offer of cars (ŠKODA PLUS - verified cars) they advertised a Škoda Superb with L&K equipment, 4x4, DSG year 2016 with a mileage of 57,333 km, stating that it is a car in good condition after repair, but with an extended warranty of 100,000 km or 5 years of age of the car. So I contacted this branch saying I would like to look at the vehicle. Naturally, I was also interested in the extent of the mentioned damage and subsequent repairs to the vehicle. I was assured that it was only a case of "failure to brake" at the intersection (repair price around 130,000,-) and the vehicle was repaired at an authorized service center while meeting the strict criteria that, as stated in the ŠKODA PLUS program, are imposed on all these vehicles . After further communication with the branch, I was informed that there is actually no warranty on this vehicle, but otherwise that everything is valid and fits as stated in the ad. So I set out on a journey to see a really spectacular spectacle for more than 500,000 NOK. The car was parked in the farthest corner of the entire area, not washed, there was green dirt on the chrome strips on the doors and in the folds (probably from how the car had been standing for quite a few months), scratches on the hood, doors, bumpers, the inside of the vehicle was dirty, "something went wrong" with the rear doors, because they blew unpleasantly and loudly when driving, the moldings and rubber on the doors were peeling off, there was water and other things in the tail light. As they say - beauty replaced splendor. My jaw dropped and I was a little unsure whether this was really the vehicle from the ad. The gentleman who attended to us was obviously out of the loop (or under orders from management to keep quiet) and just shrugged his shoulders. When I asked him to produce the documents regarding the damage to the vehicle, he brought a nice stack of papers. The vehicle had been replaced (obviously not professionally), and the condition also corresponded to this. What saddens me the most about the whole thing is the fact that the ŠKODA PLUS program is promoted everywhere, where there should really be a guarantee of quality, but in this case it was not. If I hadn't directly asked about the extent of the damage, I probably would have understood that I should have been better informed and the seller just tried (I have no doubt there will be another jackass who buys this vehicle after me) but in this case there was an obvious and deliberate cover up facts that cast a completely different light and price on the vehicle. I considered for a long time whether it makes sense to write a review, but given that I spent half a day in the car and this is the ŠKODA PLUS program, which has already been mentioned several times, it would be good to at least avoid this in the future.
recentReviews.author: Martin Šicner

recentReviews.text: We drove 100km here to check out a Superb in very good condition after repairs. We preferred to call to ask about the extent of the accident... everything was fine, just a slight brake failure on the rear of the car. Upon arrival, at first glance, a pretty nice car, here and there, cosmetic, proportional to the age of the car. We insisted on the seller that we wanted to know the detailed extent of the damage after the accident, they did not show us photos, they did not have them (the car was their company car and they also did the repairs) after listing everything that had been changed, we found out that the car had been smashed on the back side and everything had been replaced, including the airbag... they lifted the car on a jack and I am amazed that they can offer such a car under the statement "very good after an accident". The car was rusty in the repair areas (fender), the front sill was dented including damage and cracking of the paint, the front shock absorbers were leaking. The arrogant seller just kept quiet and thought about his own business. In my opinion, the car repair was done quite unprofessionally, otherwise it wouldn't be so rusty a few years after the repair. Maybe next time it would be better to just say on the phone what was being repaired, rather than dragging people all the way to a well-repaired car...

recentReviews.text:
Today, February 19, 2026, I was at the DRUPOL car repair shop with my 3rd generation Škoda Octavia because I discovered a problem with the panoramic sunroof – the window would not close on its own. So I drove to the branch in Beroun, where after I told them that I could not close the panoramic sunroof, I ran into the service manager who almost laughed at me, saying that it was not his problem. Another employee was standing nearby, listening to everything as I explained what the problem was.

Despite this message, both gentlemen were almost unable to go and look at the car, which was visible through the shop window from where we were standing. When I asked them to kindly go and at least look, they finally went. They both looked at the open window and asked me to open it all the way. That worked, but when the window was about to reach the full closing stage, it seemed to hit a bump and would not close completely.

The gentlemen told me to leave the car there and they would take a look at it. However, I needed the car at that moment. I thought that a little lubrication would be enough and everything would be fine. However, even after several repetitions, nothing changed and the window remained ajar.

Finally, we agreed that I would come the next morning and the service station would take a look at the car. After returning home, I searched the Internet for the most common causes of a sunroof malfunction. I found several "tricks" on how to reset the panoramic roof system - just hold down the button for retracting the curtain for 10 seconds. I tried that and at the same time moved the control wheel down, which unlocked the window closing lock. The window then closed without any force, unlike the situation at the service station, where the two gentlemen in front of me pushed it towards the roof with their hands to close it.

Their approach struck me as absolutely unprofessional. It was recommended to replace the entire roof, or to carry out extensive cleaning and other interventions, although the solution was very simple. All you had to do was hold down one button and the problem was solved. The gentlemen would then charge who knows how many thousand for the repair, while they would not do anything. They pressed one button ...

The whole matter was ended today with a phone call to the information of the Škoda DRUPOL company, where I asked to be transferred to the service manager. I hope that my experience will reach the responsible person of the service manager and management in general.

This is a very unpleasant experience and I will never use this service again in the future.

Thank you for nothing.

They give me an estimate of the price upon arrival and charge me double the price upon departure, without doing anything extra? I wanted to play a program that the radio was not reading my flash drive properly. They didn't do anything about it, that they could do it (oddly enough, the price was double what it would be if they did it) and change the oil. They drained the oil and when I drove 30 km a few days later to kindly suck it out again, they refused to do it right away (of course, I would have waited a while until a technician was available). I guess I can learn how to suck out oil in 30 minutes according to YouTube, but they need an extra day here and I'll bet they'd try to pass it off as a new job. So I'll be driving back and forth because of their sloppiness and they won't even apologize? Seriously, which of you drivers didn't learn to use an oil dipstick in a driving school? I don't know anyone who would tell me that they go there repeatedly for after-warranty service. And I'm not talking about the time. Does a scheduled oil change really take more than three hours? Probably not, unless you invoice it.

recentReviews.text: I would like to thank Auto Ševčík Prachatice for the help they provided us, even though they were officially closed. We were returning from a winter holiday in Šumava (15.2.) and on the way our right front wheel started to squeak very loudly, sometimes not at all and sometimes very loudly, but rather constantly. We tried calling Škoda assistance, where they advised us that it could be a stone caught in the wheel. We tried to find someone who could help us and we called nearby service stations to see if anyone would answer the phone on their day off. We also called the contact for the Auto Ševčík towing service, after a while a very helpful gentleman answered us, who promised to help us, even though it was a day off. We drove carefully to the service station, whistling, sometimes quite loudly, and there the technician lifted our car, removed the wheel and there was indeed a stone there and not just one, it had gotten there from the road gritting in Šumava, where they don't salt it. We even did a test drive together and then drove home in peace. Thanks again for your help! I recommend this service! 🙂

recentReviews.text:
AVOID AUTOSERVICE ČERVENÝ IN MARIÁNSKÝ LÁZNÍ!!!

This is a group of amateurs who only improvise when repairing cars, in order to charge Škofina as much as possible for non-existent repairs and non-existent replacement of spare parts!!

ATTENTION !!!:
Autoservis Červený technicians routinely install NON-ORIGINAL PARTS in cars!

ATTENTION!!!
When handing over the car for repair, the service technician Mr. Richter tries to fraudulently have you sign several documents, on which there is already a pre-calculated amount to pay for the repair in the amount of 20,000 CZK ????! (even!!!), without even seeing the car, and most importantly, he does not consult with you about the defect and of course does not inform you about what you are signing!!!

When asked why such a high amount is ready to be signed? Mr. Richter replied that: "it's because they don't know what's wrong with the car yet"!

Good fraudulent practices of the Auto Červený car service, that (next time they'll push you with 100,000 CZK for your signature??? ;-) ) ?

The car was handed over to the service on March 25, 2019 at around 8 am with a defect:

The car reported to the owner that the AdBlue fluid needed to be topped up. The owner topped up the fluid. The car did not recognize the top-up and still reports that the AdBlue fluid needs to be topped up - range 1,000 km.

The unprofessional technicians of the Auto Červený car service struggled with the repair for almost 3 days, and admitted on the phone that they didn't know how to do the repair.

Finally, the technicians replaced the AdBlue tube (dosing line to the injection) and unnecessarily disassembled the engine! Subsequently, they installed the timing belt incorrectly (shifted it by 1 tooth), and also broke off the fuel tube, then primitively and amateurishly adjusted the fuel injection hose (see photo). They charged 3,625 CZK for this damage to the car.

On March 27, 2019 at around 2:30 p.m., the car owner asked the service technician, Mr. Richter, for the phone number of the company's management. Mr. Richter did not comply with the customer, saying that he "does not know the phone number", and thus the car owner could not agree with anyone on further steps regarding the repair of the said car to mutual satisfaction.

When the service technician, Mr. Richter, was handing over the car to the owner and leaving the service, he turned off the engine 3 times!
As a technician, he cannot recognize a poorly installed timing belt by ear?!
With such a serious defect, they will easily let you leave the service!!!

In light of this fact, the car owner, with risk and concern, immediately drove to another authorized Škoda service.
However, during the journey, diesel began to spray from the car engine under the hood and the car became undriveable. The cause was discovered and the car owner put the fuel hose back in the right place himself for the time being!

The unprofessional intervention of the Auto Červený technicians was confirmed in another service!!!

In this service, the owner paid for the damage caused by the Auto Červený technicians:

1. for the correct installation of the timing belt 8,813 CZK!
2. for the repair of the fuel pipe and the replacement of non-original parts with original ones 15,313 CZK!

The total damage caused by the Červený car service technicians to the owner of the Škoda Superb is 24,126 CZK!

I can substantiate all these facts factually, with photo documentation, documentary evidence and testimonies!!

The car owner will seek compensation for the damage caused by Autoservis Červený!

They "checked it out" and nothing was found. When the car was then brought into a different shop for a regular check up they found the pucks immediately.

Maximum satisfaction – worth the trip even from Prague!

I took my 10-year-old Fabia 1.2 TSI 81kw to this car repair shop in Liberec from Prague – and I definitely don’t regret it! It was a preventive replacement of the timing belt, water pump and spark plugs, but the main reason was my request for a really careful and honest approach, which is not often seen these days. And here I found it.

The car was in perfect order after all the work, without the slightest reservations. Moreover, they pleasantly surprised me – the engine was perfectly cleaned, the interior and exterior looked and smelled like new. I haven’t seen my car in such condition for a long time. Truly professional work from A to Z.

The price exactly matched the previous agreement and the work performed. Fair dealings, a human approach and visible joy from a job well done. That’s how it should be!

I highly recommend it to everyone who wants to have their car cared for in a way that makes sense – even if they have to drive a little. It’s worth it.

And a big thank you to my friend Dan Vodrážek for the great recommendation and mediation – he was absolutely spot on!

recentReviews.text: Good day, I bought a car in Gremos 2 years ago. I had an unnamed company put foil on the pillars and roof. After hitting a stone, I took the car to Gremos to replace the windshield. After taking over the car, the foil was cut on both sides by a string and we also found out that the entire window did not fit and was glued poorly. After difficult negotiations with Gremos, we had them re-glue the window so that it would fit. After taking over, we found out again that this time they had scratched the sun protection strip on the window. We would not have solved it any other way, but not even 3 days later the window cracked. So we had the window repaired by another company, where we were told that the previous glass was glued down terribly. We were surprised that the authorized service behaved so arrogantly towards its customers. It was my second and last car bought here and unfortunately I will not show up for any other service here. recentReviews.text:
interested in buying a Volkswagen Passat - inquiry by email, communication with Mr. Lízr was fine, however, the vehicle, which is new, only has a two-year warranty and the factory warranty cannot be extended because the vehicle is not from the Czech Republic, you can only buy a warranty from another unnamed company for money, for a considerable amount. Communication with the dealer was standard. The vehicle was offered for a counter-account and here a problem arose, when the offered amount did not correspond to the condition of the vehicle and was conditioned by the condition that the OA would only buy back the original wheels, which they do not have anywhere on the website in the conditions in the buy-back offer section.

During my personal visit to the showroom, when I still went to look at the mentioned vehicle, there was another dealer who works as a salesman at the showroom with Mr. Lízr, who did not show much interest in selling cars, he only asked if I needed anything and when I said that I would look at the Passat on display, he just nodded and did not further inquire whether I needed any information about the vehicle. I asked about two other vehicles during the time at the showroom, since they have a total of 3 new Passats, to which I was only told that the other two were on the outdoor exhibition area behind the showroom and before I went there, I should close the trunk of the vehicle being viewed. There was no mention of the possibility that the salesman would take me there.

I also visited the used Skoda showroom opposite, here Mr. Randus showed me a vehicle that I liked, he went through the vehicle records with me, everything was standard, just very quickly, but professionally. It was clear that they are going for the volume of vehicles sold, when they have very full capacity of the used car showrooms and want to sell and do business. So for me a nice 4 stars, but the interest could be better from some employees, better and more flexible. Nevertheless, thanks to Mr. Randus for his time and Mr. Lízr for email communication.

recentReviews.text:
We highly recommend Škoda - Auto Hybeš!
Even half a year ago, my husband and I would not have thought that we would go for a new Kodiaq, but it happened 🙏🏻😁 and we are very happy that we chose Škoda - Auto Hybeš, Dřenice to fulfill our dream and we had the honor of working with Mr. Tomáš Suchý! 🙏🏻
When we started thinking about buying a new family car, we spread the so-called "networks" and contacted several dealers to obtain more detailed information. The initial conversation by email with Mr. Suchý gave us the feeling that our journey would lead to Dřenice. The first phone call followed, the second, maybe even the third 😆, but after the first one we knew that Mr. Suchý was the person with whom we wanted to make our dream come true.

Professional, but at the same time human approach, helpfulness, communication from start to finish, professional knowledge, everything was top notch! Mr. Tomáš is the right person in the right place!

The "D" day came very quickly. We went for our new darling in two months, which we really didn't expect 😍 and it was a great morning. Mr. Suchý spent a lot of time with us, went through absolutely everything with us, from A to Z. There wasn't a question he didn't know the answer to.
There was an awful lot of information, I would need to repeat the entire training, but that's another problem. 😅
I can't forget Mrs. Kasalová, whose behavior and demeanor were very pleasant. Handling everything necessary was absolutely without a problem.

I apologize for the style, but for many people, buying a new car is a big event and it's not just about coming, paying and leaving. ☝🏻💸🚗

If we ever buy a new Škoda car again (and I already believe that it's quite possible 😅), it will definitely be at Škoda - Auto Hybeš!
Once again, a big THANK YOU to Tomáš Suchý, it was great! 🙏🏻❤️
Zuzana S.

Zde se mě z prvu ujal kolega, později kolegyně, která mě měla původně v řešení. Byla to pro mě nepříjemná situace, kdy jsem jel domů po náročné noční službě. Těšil jsem se spíše domů na odpočinek a né na další nepříjemnou věc, kterou budu muset řešit.
Musím říct, že i když to byla nepříjemná věc, tak jsem se setkal s velmi milým a profesionální přístupem obou zaměstnanců. Nakonec to nebylo až tak závažné jak se na první pohled zdálo. Velmi jsem si ulevil. Auto jsem si mohl brzy vyzvednout. Navíc jako malé bolestné jsem dostal od servisu sadu skleniček na víno.
Tímto bych chtěl poděkovat nejen oběma zaměstnancům, ale i zbytku týmu techniků, kteří se na práci na mém autě podíleli.
Děkuji.
PS: Už vím kam příště do servisu, kde se mi a mému autu dostane milé a profesionální péče.

recentReviews.text: I am satisfied with the work and the negotiations. What I am not satisfied with is the amount and form of the bill. I take the fact that a regular oil change is significantly more expensive than in a small service station as a tax on marketing and other overhead costs of a large brick-and-mortar service station. However, almost 100% of the total increase was achieved, for example, by the fact that 4 liters of charged oil were allegedly filled into the engine from 1-liter bottles that were 25% more expensive than in KellyAuto (even after a 10% discount) - the commonly used 4-liter package was probably not available at the time... It almost seems that the breakdown into several small items in the bill was used only to mask the high difference in prices.
recentReviews.author: Michal Stepanek

recentReviews.text:
I don't usually leave reviews, but here it's no different. What Louda doesn't solve and they tell you that "they don't know how to solve it further" and then they don't call you back, even if you write 2 emails and the lady promises that the technician will take care of it tomorrow and call him back, you wait a week and nothing, at Auto Dryml they will find the problem and fix the problem within 30 minutes of waiting. One of the sensors falsely reported an obstacle, Louda did a diagnosis and said that everything was fine and they also checked the sensor mechanically and found nothing, and that if the problem persists I should call them back and send them a video of the moment at which the sensor falsely reports an obstacle. I sent an email with the video, no one called me back for a week, even though I reminded them. So I took the car to Dryml, where they immediately saw that the license plate was low and also found that the car had outdated software, which is why the display sometimes does not respond to touch after an incorrect message from the sensor, they took care of everything while I waited.

Today I called Louda, where of course they apologized profusely that the mechanic could not reach the lady for a week, and I also asked whether the previous mechanic had not seen the outdated software in the diagnostics and I was told that it had not and it was suggested to me to replace the two sensors as the next step, whether the same sensor would continue to report an obstacle incorrectly...

I would like to say that if the mechanic is not able to recognize that the license plate is interfering with the sensor and outdated software and the service does not even hear from you, it is a picture of the service, its approach to work and the customer in itself and I definitely do not recommend Louda.

When handing the car back to me, I have discovered they did not check the above, their argument being that it was not written on the request. They asked for video recording of such behaviour. Worse, they also padded the bill by charging me for screenwash liquid, 3 units (liters? for 153 Kc). There is no way they could fit 3 liters into the car now, because I have filled it up just a couple of days ago, and did not drive almost at al since then. recentReviews.text:
The used vehicle I purchased here was sold to me without a valid technical inspection, without me being informed of this fact when I bought it.

The invalidity of the MOT was confirmed to me only after I discovered this fact myself and contacted the seller. The seller subsequently acknowledged that it was an administrative error on their part. He offered to provide a new MOT, but the seller is located approximately 80 km from my place of residence and there was no solution as to how I could legally transport the car to Prostějov without an MOT, which I consider to be an unprofessional approach.

The discount provided upon purchase was presented as a discount on a flawless vehicle, not as compensation for the missing technical inspection, yet it was subsequently used as an argument against further solutions.

I recommend that future buyers thoroughly verify the validity of the MOT before taking over the vehicle.

After publishing the above review, the seller contacted me, apologized, and offered to cover the costs of the technical inspection, which he promptly carried out. I appreciate that they evaluated the situation retrospectively and decided to resolve it correctly.

recentReviews.text: Imagine that you have a Fabia under extended warranty, which suddenly starts poorly, does not idle, and runs unstable. You contact your service center, where they will gladly accept you with an unknown defect. Even if they did not give you an estimate of the cost, you do not deal with it. Your car is under warranty. After a few days, you will learn from the service center that they are still looking for a defect and that they have their own procedures given by the Škoda – Auto methodology. After more than a week, they call you that the fault is low-quality fuel and that it will cost you over nine thousand crowns. I cannot help but feel that they are learning at the service center with our money. If they had more experience, they could have drained the fuel for a fraction of the price with these symptoms and decided on the meaning of a more expensive procedure. Or maybe we can be glad that they did not look for the defect longer according to the Škoda methodology? Or was it their own methodology? This is not how they build a good reputation for themselves or the brand.

I would like to share my very positive experience with AUTODRUŽSTVE ZNOJMO, specifically the Dacia dealer Mr. Krčál. Although we did not end up buying the vehicle because the desired model was not immediately available, his approach was absolutely exemplary.
From the first contact, the communication with Mr. Krčál was very pleasant and professional. He willingly lent us a test car, answered all questions regarding Dacia cars and tried to understand our needs and preferences. He devoted enough time to us and presented the available models and their specifications in detail.
I especially appreciated the transparency and honesty regarding delivery times. He clearly told us that the car we had chosen was in short supply, but he still made every effort to meet our requirements. I did not feel any pressure to buy another model, which is unfortunately often the case with dealers.
Overall, I had a really great feeling about the negotiations with Mr. Krčál. Although the deal ultimately did not take place, Mr. Krčál willingly offered his services in the future, e.g. with car servicing, etc. I would like to thank him for his kind and helpful approach.
I would like to recommend the AUTODRUŽSTVO ZNOJMO dealership to anyone who is considering buying a Dacia. Their professionalism, willingness and fair approach are something that is not often seen these days.

I don't often give reviews, but the behavior of the car center when I was interested in a used car from Škoda Plus under the IVOCAR car center in Ivančice does not allow me to do otherwise. It was a Škoda Superb Combi LaK, 2017, price 400 thousand CZK.
When we found a car under the Škoda Plus brand, I believed that it was a guarantee of a certain quality. But I was terribly wrong. Information about the car was INTENTIONALLY concealed on the car, or rather the photo documentation did not contain all the defects, just as the advertisement did not state anything about the car being demolished and repaired. Neither the photo documentation nor the information in the advertisement contained a word about any of the following:
1. the car had a cracked heated front glass (replacement is quite expensive), yet the advertisement did not mention this, according to the technician there was a "dot" on the window - about 2 cm,
2. the car was painted on one side (according to the technician on site), when using a paint gauge it was clear that in some places the standard value of about 150 was exceeded about 8 times (somewhere 1000 was measured, somewhere even unmeasurable values), so it was not painting, but an unprofessionally performed repair including puttying,
3. on the trunk door, as well as above the rear light on the bodywork there were traces of corrosion (rusty bearings) - it was not surface rust, a replacement or repair would be necessary, which someone had already attempted on at least the rear door (it was partially painted),
4. the car was on summer tires, which were absolutely worn out and unusable (again not a word in the ad),
5. scuffed front bumper and other "less significant defects".

We were repeatedly assured by the technician on site that for this price the car is in excellent condition, and that he did not know what else to expect from a car that has 190 thousand km.

Even before we found out all this, we asked the technician whether it would be possible to reserve the car until the next morning (we were at the inspection around 15:00 the previous day), that we would need a mechanic to come and look at the car, because it is not a car for a few thousand. We were immediately warned that this is not possible and it is necessary to resolve (buy) immediately on the spot, because the reservation is not possible (although the previous interested party was able to wait from Monday to Wednesday, as they decide).
I would like to warn everyone against buying a car at this auto center, because even the Škoda brand obviously no longer means quality. And at the same time, I hope that the review will not go unnoticed by the management at this car center.

recentReviews.text: I bought a car here in May 2022, year of manufacture 2016, mileage 65,000 km, advertised as a proven car in the Das Welt program. 3 months after the sale, at a mileage of 65,000 km, a seized gearbox, a humming air conditioning compressor, a malfunctioning ad-blue pump, and a non-functional window regulator were found. Your solution and approach were appalling. After I refused a refund for the car, some defects were repaired (the others were no longer recognized because I applied for them additionally after consulting with an authorized service center, but still within the statutory six-month period), where the highlight of everything was that after more than 3 months of repairs, the car was returned to me with a messed-up geometry and I was told that I had to do this at my own expense, that it was not in the manufacturer's service procedure when installing the gearbox. If you are lucky enough to get a car that is in order, everything will probably be ok, but as soon as you have to solve a problem, Auto Jarov mainly cares about its financial interests at the expense of customer satisfaction. Especially the head of the buyout, Mr. Kubinec, seems to me like an incompetent amateur who is either unable to ensure that he receives relevant information from his partners and subordinates in a timely manner or is simply lying and lying and is unable to provide true and up-to-date information to customers. From his behavior and subsequent solutions, you can feel that he is primarily interested in his own economic benefit (in short, so that the Auto Jarov company can get out of the problem that arose from the inconsistent inspection of the car as cheaply as possible). It does not occur to them to lend you a replacement vehicle for a symbolic price, nor will they clean it and hand it over to you dirty and with a messed-up geometry. In short, they will not do anything extra to make up for their inconsistency. They will only do what they have to to keep you quiet and so that it is not worth it for you to sue them for the remaining offenses. I definitely can't recommend it for myself.

recentReviews.text:
With time and emotions calmed down, I would like to give a rather negative review for the needs of future clients of the service and for the staff to think about.
The car was towed to the service with the assistance of Škoda Assistance for a flat tire on Thursday, August 28, 2025, in the early evening. The next day, I waited until about 1 p.m. to hear how the car was. Finally, I contacted the service myself and learned that work had not started on the car because they did not know whose car it was and what to do with it. And that the order had not been properly placed. They said that I would need to show up. But in the end, it was enough to take a photo of a small TP and send it. The initial information was that the tire was not in stock and that it would be "sometime on Tuesday". It was a precarious situation because I was in the area on vacation with the children and we needed to get home to a place about 2.5 hours away on Sunday. When I asked about a replacement car or a tow truck, I received a negative answer.
They really helped me only at the second authorized service in Šumperk, Fortex, where they recommended the nearby Pneucentrum Matěj. They actually had compatible tires there and Auto Hégr agreed to fit them if I brought them. In the meantime, however, they themselves asked the same dealer for the tires and offered to resell them to me. However, with a surcharge of about 3100 CZK/pc (I needed 2 pcs so that each side would be the same). They charged 3300 CZK for the tire change, cleaning the latex from the gluing kit and handling it to the workshop (45 min) and I was told that they had worked hard. The difference between the tire service price and their pre-sale, which they themselves asked about, they said that they simply had it that way. Unfortunately, the whole thing still affects me that I encountered lazy staff at the service station, who even tried to take advantage of my desperate situation. And just as an aside to the 1* review here: I really recommend a longer company T-shirt and better fitting trousers for the gentleman. After all, someone who works with people shouldn't reveal their stomach and backside. Regardless of their physique, I'm not judging them at all! And a more accommodating approach or at least consistent information would certainly help - no way, we don't have it, we won't make it, you can understand on a Friday afternoon. But the experience here was "so in the end we can, but we'll charge extra for both the material and the work" - the original information was approx. 2500 vs. the final 3300.
From my point of view, this is a disgrace for the Škoda service network, especially if our car is now 3 months old (then 2 months old) and the mobility guarantee is valid. I also compare it to the excellent behavior of Fortex in Šumperk and Gremos in Liberec.
In the end, I was glad that I drove away in my own car, but the customer experience was simply miserable. Thank you anyway and I wish you improvements in the future and satisfied clients.

recentReviews.text:
Edit 3.10.2024
Well, it's clear that you'll stand your ground, you're pretending to be an authorized service, but that's all. The handover of the car was as follows, I was supposed to sign the papers right at the desk in the office, then the keys were handed over and the car was parked somewhere by the wall. Mr. Petržela said that everything was repaired and since it was hail, I checked the bodywork, it wouldn't have occurred to me that I had to check the interior after the employees from the supposedly "authorized service" to see if they installed everything correctly and further that you forgot to deliver the part... If the part had been delivered there, I would of course pay for it. After the complaints, I was concerned about the lost time and the worries associated with it. He thought that you would confront this and we would come to some agreement. And yes, I refused the replacement car, I have another one. As for the free repair, that's laughable, since it's work within the scope of the complaint.
Everything corresponds to the quality of your service, which is insufficient. What is surprising is that I had to visit the service in Horní Lán in Olomouc to be told exactly what was wrong and then I contacted your manager, who called back to Olomouc to ask what part it was. That says it all.
I had my car repaired elsewhere and was satisfied and I consider this matter closed.

As for the statement from Škoda Auto, I expected such a statement when, after the emergencies since the beginning of August, the statement came at the beginning of October. It is clear how much the entire group values ​​its customers.

If you still have something on your mind that is my fault, then tell me here.

I am sending you the complaint that I sent to Škoda Auto, described below. Although the answer from you will be that this is not the case. I want to share my experience with your service.

On July 1, 2024, I took my car to this service for an "element - hail" insurance claim and on July 10, 2024, I went to pick up the repaired car. Unfortunately, it never occurred to me to check my car after the repair, and by that I mean, for example, the roof of the vehicle, the covers in the upholstery handles, the sun visors that were correctly installed...

After about ten days, I started to find out about the defects in the service. I found incorrectly installed sun visors, installed covers and dirt, probably from a rubber mallet, in all the handles.

Then I started to have the car manually and when it came to the roof, I couldn't help but be surprised. When I pressed the roof slightly, it started to sag.

This made me very uneasy and I contacted the service manager, Mr. Petržela, by email along with a video. The service manager wrote to me the next day that this was a normal condition for the group's cars.

I didn't think so, so I visited the authorized VW service in Olomouc, Horní Lán, and thanks to them, I found out that the anti-vibration material was not glued to the roof in the Prostějov service.

That day, I also visited the service in Prostějov, where my car was being repaired. I asked the manager to go look at the roof, argued for a while that it was fine. Finally, he told me that he would contact the Olomouc service and get advice. The next day, he emailed me that I was right. He offered to deliver the missing material to me, saying that they would install it for free "the installation of this part is not difficult and for me it is a ridiculous offer" and I don't want a rented car.

On July 23, 2024, I visited the service with the idea that I would like the service to take a stand and buy the material and install it at their own expense. I visited the service 3 times, it cost me a lot of nerves and time, and I also had to take time off work to deal with the complaint.

The service manager did not agree, so I wanted to talk to my superior. I called the director, unfortunately he was away and on the phone he told the manager that the offer from the service would be enough for me and not to let me on the phone, that he did not want to talk to me. Well, that is really a negotiation with a customer. I got his contact information and immediately wrote him an email that I would not be against a reasonable agreement, but unfortunately no response.
The missing part costs 2900 CZK.
I was very surprised by the approach of the authorized Škoda service to the whole thing. And this behavior really does not make a good name for itself. Of course, I will not recommend the service anywhere and would rather avoid it altogether.
I also took pictures of the car when I handed it over, both outside and inside.

I should note that it was not some Chinese product, but the best that can be bought on the Czech market. I was told that they do not install non-original accessories, which I would understand. I regretted that I received the camera as a gift and wanted to put it into operation, whereupon the reception technician promptly replied that I should tell the person who gave it to me about the installation and that's it. I have the opportunity to compare some of the cars I have already owned, but I have never experienced this in an authorized service station.

recentReviews.author: Žanda Juříčková

recentReviews.text: I want to share my experience. We had been choosing a family car for a long time. And when we finally decided that we wanted a Skoda Kodiaq, the search began. We found many, but one caught our attention, among the used Skoda Plus cars in Frýdek Místek. I called the branch and said that I was from Prague East and that I would like to come and see it. Mr. Baier's behavior was fantastic, he told us everything about the car, he didn't hide anything. We went to Moravia on the 2nd day to see it. We were completely disarmed by how nice the gentlemen treat their customers. The test drive lasted over 20 minutes. The gentleman even took us on a broken road. When I asked if I could run diagnostics, I was given time to go through everything and check it. The Kodiaq completely absorbed us. And the contract was already being drawn up. No less, after driving 610 km, an adblue error appeared. I called Mr. Baier and there was no problem, I brought the car back and on the way to the service the magnetic ring on the bearing broke and all possible errors started popping up. After returning the car, I received a replacement car without a single word. After 3 days I went back to get the finished car. I haven't experienced such behavior in a long time. I have to say that I recommend not 10/10 but 20/10. I was amazed at how the gentlemen tried to do their best to satisfy me. And if I had to buy a car again, or recommend it, I would only buy it from the gentlemen from Frýdek. Thank you to everyone who was involved. You are my friends!!!! Many thanks again.
recentReviews.author: jiri perautka

recentReviews.text:
I have mixed feelings about the Autodružstvo FM service. Sometimes the approach to the customer is on a high level, sometimes unfortunately the opposite. As a paying customer who leaves a lot of money in an authorized service, I would expect professional and helpful communication at every contact and also an active effort to solve any problems — not only after my repeated warning. After all, I don't have my car serviced in a garage somewhere called "Pepík".

I give three stars mainly because of the fluctuating communication. Otherwise, I rate the level of service itself as decent. Hopefully, on my next visits, everything will be fine and 5 stars.

recentReviews.text:
Autodružstvo - Provozovna Český Těšín / SKODA SUPERB SPORT.

We are openly giving one star because of the careless replacement of brake pads, which are probably the most important part of a car maintenance service. They claimed to have checked them, but in reality they were never checked. After only 400 kilometers, the brake pads were worn out - should we expect such a result after a service? In my opinion, this is not a reputable car service. This problem appeared on my car and we experienced the same problem with our second vehicle at their workshop. Moreover, they called us in complete dismay, asking why we gave them one star and how the owner of this workshop effectively insulted us for daring to do so. I advise everyone to carefully check their service to make sure that the work they charge you for was actually done. They will not cheat me three times. Shameless liars. And by the way, reviews are there to warn people when things are not right. We have purchased more than 10 cars from this workshop. During Uncle Penčák's time, everything was done correctly and professionally. However, since the younger Penčák took over, the quality of services has declined.
**********************************************************************
Openings are given one star because of the niedbałej wymyany klocków hamulcowych, which are probably the most important element of the service service of the car. Twierdzili że bóly sprodzjane, but in reality they never saw them. After only 400 kilometers klocki hamulkowe złoty zużyte - czy taki wynik poża oczekiwać po serwisie? Moi zdaniem to nie jest renomowany serwis samochodowy. The problem appeared in my car, and the same problem we had in our car. What's more, they called us from przerażeniem, pytając, dlaczego daliśmy im jednje gwiazdkę, and the owner of this workshop effectively insulted us for daring to do it. I advise wszystkim to carefully check swoje usługi serwisowe to make sure that the work for which they are paid has really been done. Mnie nie oszukają trzy razy. Zawstydzający kłamcy. A propos, reviews są tam po to, by ostrzegać ludzi, gdy coś jest nie w zódznó. We bought more than 10 cars including warsztacie. Za czasów wuja Penčák wszystko było wykonywane correctly and professionally. On the one hand, since the moment of przejęcia przez młozzego Penčák, the quality of services has dropped.
